Chromium Daily Summaries

Latest Chromium commits summarized daily

📅 View Weekly Summaries →

2026-05-28

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-28

Overview

This summary covers the 732 commits merged into the Chromium main branch on 2026-05-28, with 547 identified as relevant for developer impact and system stability. The daily activity is bounded by commit (72eea7e) and (72eea7e).

Security & Memory Safety

  • Enhanced browser resilience by migrating navigation internals to MiraclePtr, patching TOCTOU vulnerabilities in extensions, and enforcing mandatory Permissions Policy checks for Gamepad/Haptics Mojo interfaces. (e6f8ab6) (db75f6e) (d5c6640)
  • Implemented UAF mitigations in media/windowing subsystems, including fixed lifetimes for content analysis uploaders and new ReallocProtectedIterator usage. (38c09cd) (33b8433)
    SecurityMedia

Rendering & Web Platform

  • Stabilized new CSS capabilities including light-dark() support for images, image() color notation, and background-clip: border-area. (a2a9212) (13f6f54)
    BlinkCSS
  • Advancing WebNN project with infrastructure for dedicated compiler utility processes and strict NPU driver version validation to prevent stability issues. (74c0abd) (b111946)
    AIGPU

Infrastructure & Build

  • Accelerated build pipelines by enabling remote execution via Siso for resource generation and modularizing webui components into source_set targets. (4763209) (d3edf2d)
    BuildInfra
  • Standardized test synchronization by migrating away from RunUntilIdle() to modern primitives like TestFuture and RunUntil to improve suite reliability. (003b355) (ef0c77f)
    TestingAsh

2026-05-27

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-27

Overview

A total of 761 commits were recorded on the main branch for this day. Following analysis of 586 relevant changes, the engineering focus was on security hardening, performance optimization, and architectural modularization. No breaking changes to public stable APIs were identified. The first commit was (b6dc005) and the last was (ce8143d).

Security & Memory Safety

  • Enhanced memory safety by adding validation for dma-buf sizes during Vulkan imports and correcting integer truncation errors in media parsers. (9185cf5) (2ef2134)
    SecurityGPUMedia
  • Hardened URL handling and navigation security, including blocking potentially dangerous local file schemes in Handoff and disabling HTTPS-to-HTTP fallback. (12e2551) (6a44eca)
    SecurityNavigation

Rendering, Blink & Media

  • Improved rendering performance and standards compliance, including updates to CSS @supports, refined PaintArtifactCompositor caching, and cleanup of unused metrics. (d9f40f9) (ca29db2) (e64bae8)
    BlinkCSSPerformance
  • Optimized H.265 decoding by avoiding non-decodable frame storage and added support for automatic unspoken punctuation in Web Speech. (e1a2b97) (71a62e4)
    Media

Build, Infrastructure & Architecture

  • Continued architectural decoupling of browser features, including migrating components to SplitCompatService and moving string resources to ash/. (cfb8265) (f1a431f)
    InfraBuildAsh
  • Optimized build system performance by refining Siso support for remote execution and enabling the mold linker for Android arm64 builds. (5d24809) (e4ee266)
    BuildAndroidInfra

UI, UX & Feature Integrations

  • Enhanced Android UI responsiveness with asynchronous view inflation and refined Glic/Side Panel focus management. (ccc8042) (8863e3f)
    UIAndroidAI

2026-05-26

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-26

Overview

This report summarizes the activity for 715 total commits, with 539 relevant changes analyzed across the Chromium codebase. The range spans from commit (33545f4) to (6efdfba), focusing on infrastructure, platform parity, and security hardening.

Security & Privacy

  • Patched a critical vulnerability in the pageCapture API by implementing stricter URL blocking to prevent extension bypasses. (c0e1157)
    SecurityExtensions
  • Enhanced process authorization checks in WebRequest API; unauthorized extension registrations now trigger immediate renderer termination. (572cfa5)
    SecurityExtensionsBrowser
  • Hardened Device attestation for SAML by enforcing strict origin checks to mitigate confused deputy attacks. (78b42e8)
    SecurityEnterpriseCrypto

Infrastructure & Build

  • Modularized core architecture by moving 326 source files from //chrome/browser:browser to //chrome/browser/android, improving build efficiency. (cef2263)
    BuildAndroidBrowser
  • Decoupled PartitionAlloc by migrating from COMPONENT_BUILD defines to internal buildflags, enhancing portability. (49570fd)
    BuildBase
  • Optimized RBE by updating denylist.star to include various localization and build tools, reducing remote execution latency. (db67bfc) (69e926c)
    BuildInfra

Blink & Web Platform

  • Migrated modelContext API from navigator to document to align with WebMCP standards; includes a console warning for legacy access. (4ff7398)
    BlinkAIHTML
  • Enforced architecture via [NotSubclassable] IDL attribute on HTMLSubmitButtonBehavior, which now throws TypeError when subclassed. (8909196)
    BlinkHTML
  • Standardized internal naming for text-fit CSS property across Blink and layout structures. (001b5bd) (8708988)
    BlinkCSS

UI & Platform Refinement

  • Removed legacy PlusAddress infrastructure from the iOS codebase. (aec9d83)
    iOSBrowserAutofill
  • Standardized pinning behavior in UI containers by renaming Start/End to Left/Right to ensure RTL consistency. (07c82fc)
    UII18n
  • Simplified BrowserViewLayout by caching parameters at the start of the layout cycle to prevent stale state usage. (7635cb9)
    UITabs

Performance & Memory

  • Refactored SqlPersistentStore to move in-memory index updates to a background backend thread to reduce IO thread contention. (1e2584) (ecd0dd)
    StoragePerformance
  • Standardized memory usage telemetry by migrating from base::SysInfo::AmountOfPhysicalMemory to AmountOfTotalPhysicalMemory. (eb3afef)
    BasePerformance

2026-05-25

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-25

Overview

This report summarizes 102 commits from the latest Chromium development cycle. The analysis focused on identifying major breaking changes in production-ready code, confirming that no such changes were present. Key updates involve memory tracking migrations, security hardening in renderer processes, build infrastructure optimizations, and significant refactoring of editing APIs. First commit hash: (1635567); Last commit hash: (1635861).

Infrastructure & Build

  • Optimized Siso build infrastructure on Windows to reduce I/O overhead and integrated upstream updates for third-party dependencies HarfBuzz and Quiche. (1635597) (1635695) (1635707) (1635783)

Memory & Performance

  • Standardized memory reporting by migrating base::SysInfo::AmountOfPhysicalMemory to AmountOfTotalPhysicalMemory and improved memory safety with active iterator tracking in HeapVector. (1635771) (1635753) (1635607) (1635592)
    PerformanceBase

Security & Enterprise

  • Enhanced renderer security by killing processes that attempt to reuse embedding tokens and tightened Secure Payment Confirmation (SPC) parameter validation at Mojo entry points. (1635718) (1635840) (1635796)
    SecurityPayments
  • Expanded WebAuthn VDI affiliation checks and added enterprise policy controls for background fetch from Service Workers. (1635644) (1635583)
    EnterprisePolicySecurity

Blink & Rendering

  • Progressed the transition to DOM-based editing APIs by refactoring InsertTextCommand and improved layout accuracy for text-fit and full-width character handling. (1635596) (1635595) (1635593) (1635615) (1635614)
    BlinkHTMLCSS

Android & iOS Platforms

  • Refactored UI architectures to use MVC patterns for bottom sheets and stabilized various E2E test suites on mobile simulators. (1635704) (1635635) (1635713) (1635709)
    AndroidiOSUI

2026-05-24

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-24

Overview

This digest analyzes 11 commits, from (93ba0ac) to (c7d5c34). No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified. Highlights include preparatory work for Device Bound Session Credentials, ongoing development of the experimental WebMCP API, and internal improvements to media tracing and dark mode heuristics.

Security & Identity

  • Internal logic for Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) has been improved. A new GenerateTokenBindingRegistrationToken() method was added to ProfileOAuth2TokenService as a preparatory step, and token helper state is now correctly cleared on user sign-out. On iOS, sign-in logic was updated to better handle account capability changes and prevent inconsistent states. (e071a25) (d309566) (c849440)
    SecurityProfilesiOS

Blink & Web Platform

  • The experimental WebMCP (Web Machine-learning Compute Platform) API was updated to align registerTool() with recent spec changes regarding origin handling and error types. Separately, the image classification heuristic for dark mode was enhanced to better identify and preserve images with muted or mid-tone hues, preventing unwanted inversion. (a0d778a) (4903524) (210a781)
    BlinkAIUI

Media

  • The third-party AV1 codec library, libaom, was updated, incorporating a bug fix for bitstream buffer size checks. Additionally, tracing within the media/cast component was refactored from asynchronous to synchronous events to improve debugging and performance analysis. (93ba0ac) (7853374)
    MediaPerformance

Browser & UI

  • A prototype state synchronization mechanism has been implemented for the experimental 'Full WebUI Omnibox'. This allows the omnibox to persist input state across tab switches, laying the groundwork for a more robust implementation. (8cc4dee)
    UIBrowser

Infrastructure & Network

  • Routine maintenance includes an update to translation string resources (.xtb files) and a refresh of the pervasive resource patterns used by the network service to identify commonly shared web resources. (717e501) (c7d5c34)
    InfraNetworkI18n

2026-05-23

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-23

Overview

This digest covers 55 commits, spanning from (b3ed2f7) to (33b8638). Key updates include security hardening for the Extensions API, stabilization of the RequestIsReloadNavigation feature, and ongoing refactoring in ChromeOS and the WebUI toolbar. No major breaking changes were identified in this set of commits.

Security & Privacy

  • A cross-origin secret leak vulnerability in the Extensions API has been fixed. The issue occurred when a compromised renderer sent two requests with the same ID, causing network callbacks to be misrouted. The IPC boundary is now hardened to detect this, terminate the offending renderer, and prevent data leakage. (048923c)
    SecurityExtensionsNetwork
  • A potential use-after-free vulnerability in Bluetooth socket listeners on macOS has been addressed. The fix ensures the listener object outlives any pending OS notifications, preventing crashes and potential security issues. (375096a)
    SecurityBluetoothmacOS
  • The experimental Compute Pressure API has had several recent changes reverted due to concerns about it creating an information leak. Active development on this part of the feature has been stopped. (4d118b5) (d235c83) (a8f43ad) (448f7cd)
    BlinkPrivacy
  • Password fields are now more reliably redacted in screenshots by using the TrackedElement infrastructure to identify and mask sensitive areas. (421393c)
    PrivacyPasswordManager

Blink & Web Platform

  • The RequestIsReloadNavigation feature has been enabled by default, graduating it to a stable web platform feature. This allows developers to more reliably detect if a navigation was initiated by a page reload. (33b8638)
    BlinkNavigation
  • The WebAudio AudioBufferSourceHandler has been refactored to better separate the linear-interpolation rendering path, improving code clarity and preparing for future features like negative playback rates. (7e40dfa)
    BlinkMedia
  • A bug fix in the GLES2 command decoder now properly handles EXT_texture_norm16 formats during ReadPixels calls, preventing potential rendering issues or crashes. (509449e)
    GPUWebGL

UI & UX

  • Development continues on the new WebUI-based toolbar. The security icon has been refactored into a native <button> for better accessibility, and an extensions container has been added in preparation for future integration. (b889e37) (1b16115)
    UIAccessibilityExtensions
  • Several UI refinements were made as part of the "Glow Up" project, including updating vector icons to a modern rounded style and fixing an animation bug with the page info icon. (f39794b) (a88841b)
    UIThemes
  • On Android, a bug was fixed where the toolbar snapshot would have the wrong theme color when initially positioned at the bottom of the screen. (bc43a71)
    UIAndroid

ChromeOS & Ash

  • A large-scale refactoring effort continues to decouple Ash components from the higher-level browser layer. Multiple components, including OobeUIConfig, GaiaScreenHandler, and OS Settings tests, have been migrated away from ash::ProfileHelper to use lower-level APIs like ash::BrowserContextHelper. (499757b) (2676051) (15e4855) (6b2787e)
    AshChromeOS
  • A change affecting task execution order during shutdown was relanded after a series of reverts. This change aims to catch errors earlier by running tasks before the shutdown process begins, which helps stabilize tests. (2af5fa9) (eef1101)
    AshBase

Infrastructure & Refactoring

  • The build system has been updated to support TypeScript v7 via a new use_typescript_go flag, enabling more modern frontend development practices for WebUI components. (1487cbd)
    BuildInfra
  • A codebase-wide migration is underway to replace calls to the deprecated base::SysInfo::AmountOfPhysicalMemory with AmountOfTotalPhysicalMemory. This ensures more accurate memory reporting across Blink, Compositor (cc), and ChromeOS components. (40d7c8c) (e8fde96) (eafe39a)
    BaseBlinkCompositor
  • The libvpx dependency has been updated, incorporating a fix that disables VP9D_DEMACROBLOCK for small video frame dimensions to prevent potential issues. (b33840f)
    MediaBuild

2026-05-22

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-22

Overview

This report covers 703 commits on the main branch, with 507 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from commit e5b651c1fbc2f445664f0d1dda4812841ef93bd4 to c14e0f12c6566294a483f11c5e2c1d5abb31a9f4. The day was marked by a significant number of breaking API changes aimed at componentization and security hardening, the removal of the 'borderless' web app display mode, and the introduction of post-quantum key agreement in WebCrypto.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️API Removal: Buffer mapping commands (MapBufferRange, UnmapBuffer) have been removed from the public GLES2Interface. This is a security hardening measure that will break embedders using this low-level GPU API directly. (7cc1fc6)
    GPUSecurity
  • ⚠️Build & API Relocation: The Tab Strip API has been moved from chrome/browser/ui/tabs/tab_strip_api to components/browser_apis/tab_strip. Embedders must update public header paths to prevent build failures. (d10436b)
    UIBrowserBuild
  • ⚠️Web Platform API Change: The BackgroundFetchManager.fetch() API, when called from a Service Worker context, will now throw a NotAllowedError by default. This behavioral change will break existing applications unless their origin is added to a server-configurable allowlist. (2976e22)
    ServiceWorkerBlinkNetwork
  • ⚠️Web Platform Feature Removal: Support for the "borderless" value in the Web App Manifest display member and the display-mode CSS media query has been removed. Web apps relying on this feature will need to be updated. (8df25b6)
    BlinkUI
  • ⚠️Build System: The monolithic //chrome/browser/ui:ui build target has been split into smaller source_set targets. Downstream build systems with dependencies on the old target will break. (9694359)
    BuildInfra
  • ⚠️Service APIs: The Sync and Sign-in services have breaking API changes. SyncEncryptionHandler::Observer requires two new pure virtual methods, and AccountTrackerService::Initialize has been removed in favor of a new constructor signature. (31680f1) (c0a736f)
    SyncProfiles
  • ⚠️Core Browser APIs: The public method Browser::is_delete_scheduled() has been removed; developers must migrate to BrowserWindowInterface::IsDeleteScheduled(). Additionally, the return type of RenderViewContextMenu::GetBrowser() has changed from Browser* to BrowserWindowInterface*, breaking subclass overrides. (1353217) (2df2953)
    BrowserUI
  • ⚠️Android APIs: The public Java interface XrQuadSurfaceEntityHolder has been deleted, requiring migration to XrSurfaceEntityHolder. Separately, InfoBarContainer integration has been removed from core classes like TabImpl, breaking embedders who rely on its automatic management. (ed910ab) (0e10667)
    AndroidXRUI
  • ⚠️Password Manager API: The return type of PasswordManagerDriver::GetId() has been changed from a raw int to a strongly-typed password_manager::DriverId, requiring updates in code that calls this method. (f14e7b7)
    PasswordManagerAutofill
  • ⚠️Mojo & Header Changes: The printing::mojom::PrintManagerHost Mojo interface was refactored, renaming UpdatePrintSettings. Separately, i18n time formatting enums were moved from base/i18n/time_formatting.h to a new ..._types.h header. Both changes require code updates. (ea38e74) (d0f9daf)
    PrintingI18nBase
  • ⚠️WebGPU: The public SharedBuffer/TextureMemoryBegin/EndAccessDesc structs in Dawn have a new signaledValueCount member, which will break code that constructs or reads these structs directly. (7e8c9a6)
    WebGPUGPU

Security & Privacy

  • Support for the X-wing hybrid key encapsulation mechanism (ML-KEM-768 and X25519) has been implemented in WebCrypto, enabling post-quantum key agreement. (31f3485)
    CryptoBlinkSecurity
  • A new option was added to SSLServerConfig to enable server-side TLS padding, enhancing security against traffic analysis. (6eb3ab2)
    SSLNetworkSecurity
  • The mechanism for tracking extension lazy background page keepalives has been hardened to prevent underflow from a compromised renderer. (4a7596d)
    ExtensionsSecurity
  • A security enhancement now enforces that resolved download paths remain within the intended download directory, preventing symlink traversal vulnerabilities. (3cf6610)
    DownloadsSecurity
  • Support has been added to force ASLR for sandboxed child processes on Windows to improve security. (09eaeae)
    SecurityWindows

Web Platform & Blink

  • The SpellCheckCustomDictionary API is now document-scoped instead of window-scoped to prevent cross-page information leakage. (2abc282)
    BlinkPrivacy
  • The implementation for saving a video frame was moved to HTMLVideoElement and optimized to use a Blob URL instead of a Base64 data URL, reducing IPC overhead. (093208f)
    BlinkMediaPerformance
  • The use of std::span was adopted for AudioBuffer copy paths in WebAudio, improving memory safety. (c14e0f1)
    BlinkMedia
  • A crash was fixed when text-transform changes the length of text for a ::first-line pseudo-element. (5a9a0e8)
    BlinkCSS
  • A bug was fixed where the bounding box for a text selection was not correctly scaled by the layout zoom factor. (ceb1dd6)
    BlinkUI
  • A skeleton for the PerformanceScrollTiming API was added behind a feature flag, establishing the web-exposed surface for future scroll performance metrics. (0ede202)
    BlinkPerformance

Services & Core

  • A breaking change to AutofillClient::HideAutofillSuggestions was reverted due to build failures. The method was temporarily renamed and its signature changed before being restored. (ed56828) (cb7339e)
    AutofillUI
  • Logic was added to securely unmask and fill Autofill AI SPIIs (Sensitive Personally Identifiable Information), including server fetches for de-obfuscation. (f407929)
    AutofillAISecurity
  • In preparation for experimenting with ICU4X, a new IcuBridge class was introduced to abstract away calls to the ICU library. (d829192)
    I18nBase
  • The SQL-based disk cache is being improved with a new consolidated in-memory index implementation, available behind a feature flag. (ca72b14)
    NetworkStorage

UI & Platforms

  • ChromeOS: The Borealis (Steam on ChromeOS) installer WebUI and related code have been removed, deprecating the standard installation flow for the feature. (2102169)
    ChromeOSUI
  • ⚠️Chromecast: The enable_assistant GN build flag and its associated implementation files have been removed, discontinuing the feature. This is a breaking change for any build configurations that relied on this flag. (de8f5dd)
    BuildChromeOS
  • Android: The UI for immersive video playback was refactored to a new component-based architecture, and its confirmation dialog was replaced with a less intrusive Snackbar. (973ab05) (06f9b9c)
    AndroidUIMedia
  • iOS: A crash related to MemoryCredentialStore access from the Credential Provider Extension was addressed by force-disabling the Automatic Passkey Upgrade feature. (44703f6)
    iOSPasswordManagerAuth
  • A new global Ctrl-Tab MRU (Most Recently Used) switching feature was implemented, allowing users to toggle between the two most recent tabs across all browser windows. (2e673d0)
    UITabs
  • The tab loading throbber animation has been optimized to be driven by the compositor, improving performance. (cca3271)
    UICompositorPerformance

Build & Infrastructure

  • ⚠️BREAKING: A fundamental change to how Protobuf libraries are linked in component builds was made to fix ODR violations. This may break custom build configurations that interact with Chromium's Protobuf targets. (0fcbba9)
    BuildInfra
  • The kExtensionManifestV2Disabled feature flag, which has been enabled for over a year, was removed along with related dead code and tests. (e768ae9)
    ExtensionsInfra
  • A series of commits refactored CanvasResourceProvider and related classes, renaming methods to remove unneeded ForCanvas2D suffixes and simplifying the class hierarchy. (01e4c88) (a4cbf19) (aaac325) (499d6d7) (1887835)
    GPUCompositor
  • Numerous reverts were committed to fix build failures, test flakiness, and regressions, including changes to Autofill suggestions, tab loading throbbers, and WebTransport stream construction. (ed56828) (00015b9) (9379e31)
    InfraBuildTesting

2026-05-21

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-21

Overview

This digest covers 711 commits on the main branch, with 529 selected for their relevance to developers. The changes span from the first commit ((def4c19)) to the last ((4492467)) and include a significant number of breaking API changes requiring developer action.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The required Java version for building Android targets has been upgraded from Java 17 to Java 21. Developers and embedders building for Android must update their JDK. (2e2c668)
    AndroidBuild
  • ⚠️BREAKING: A new pure virtual method, GetContainerPolicyOverrideForCommit, has been added to the core content::ContentBrowserClient interface. Embedders with custom implementations must update their code. (6da6b23)
    BrowserBlink
  • ⚠️BREAKING: A new virtual method IsAutofillableElement was added to the public blink::WebAutofillClient interface. Embedders must add the new method to their implementations. (4f8eaf4)
    BlinkAutofill
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The signature of AutofillManager::Observer::OnFillOrPreviewForm has been changed to include a new trigger_field_id parameter. All observer implementations must be updated. (83568b9)
    AutofillBlink
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public Android Java classes TabModelHolder and IncognitoTabModelHolder have been removed, and TabModelHolderFactory is renamed to TabModelFactory. This breaks downstream code using these tab management classes. (5bc1da8)
    AndroidTabs
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public header system_web_app_type.h has been moved from //ash/webui/ to //chromeos/ash/components/, breaking builds for downstream projects on ChromeOS Ash. (cb83d99)
    ChromeOSAshBuild
  • ⚠️BREAKING: A new pure virtual method SendEarlyFinalBeginMainFrame was added to the FrameWidget interface to support View Transitions performance improvements, breaking custom implementations. (87c8e7c)
    BlinkCompositorUI
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The signature of RenderWidgetHostInputEventRouter::SetAutoScrollInProgress was changed and a new pure virtual method CancelAutoscroll was added to its delegate interface, breaking existing implementations. (5b15801)
    BlinkUI
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The mojom interface XRPresentationProvider::SubmitFrameDrawnIntoTexture has a new signature. Embedders must now pass an XRLayerUpdate struct. (a1ff25b)
    XRGPU
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The DesktopDisplayInfoMonitor::AddCallback method in the Remoting Host API now returns a base::CallbackListSubscription instead of void, breaking external implementations. (5fbcbfa)
    MediaUI
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public class RegisteredMemoryConsumer has been removed from base/memory_coordinator, breaking any code that was using it. (339a249)
    BasePerformance
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public static method net::CookiePartitionKeyCollection::Todo() has been removed. Use the default constructor net::CookiePartitionKeyCollection() instead. (1dfabc4)
    NetworkPrivacy
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public method FontCache::PlatformInit() has been removed from third_party/blink/renderer/platform/fonts/font_cache.h as part of a font handling refactor. (6d1611d)
    BlinkFontsmacOS
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public factory methods for the Nigori sync encryption class now require a NigoriPassKey, changing their signatures and breaking external callers. (d77c428)
    SyncCrypto
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The WebRTC StatsCollector class was refactored, changing the inheritance and public method signatures of StatsCollectingDecoder and StatsCollectingEncoder. (e73986e)
    WebRTCMedia
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The IsRoutineArgumentSupported method has been removed from the public TelemetryDiagnosticRoutinesService mojom interface on ChromeOS. (29dc6fa)
    ChromeOSTesting
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public Java property key AppMenuActionProperties.APP_MENU_SETUP_CALLBACK was removed via a revert, breaking any code that had adopted it. (00cb73d)
    AndroidUI

Web Platform & Blink

  • The setPreferredSinkId() web API is now restricted to top-level documents. Calls from within an iframe will now throw a DOMException, which is a behavioral breaking change for sites that relied on this functionality. (1e43d45)
    MediaBlinkSecurity
  • Support for spatial media fragments (xywh=...) has been added for SVG images, allowing URLs to specify a clipping rectangle. The feature is enabled behind the SvgSupportMediaFragments flag. (4d5d46a)
    BlinkMediaHTML
  • Support has been added for the CSS corner and corner-* shorthand properties. (2e4bd21)
    BlinkCSS
  • A permissions policy check for kOnDeviceSpeechRecognition has been added, preventing cross-origin iframes from using the feature unless explicitly allowed by the top-level document. (5d69e4c)
    BlinkSecurityPrivacy
  • The mechanism for detecting system font changes on macOS has been improved. The browser process now observes system notifications and informs the sandboxed renderer via IPC. (6d1611d)
    BlinkFontsmacOS
  • A potential history sniffing vector via :visited selectors has been fixed by properly clamping the alpha channel in SVG context painting and gap decorations. (bb0351b) (7aed80d)
    BlinkSecurityCSS

Security & Privacy

  • The Geolocation service was refactored to use a new GeolocationProxy to more accurately manage the lifetime of location requests, ensuring the location-in-use indicator remains active as long as a data pipe is open. (94e14bc)
    PrivacyBlink
  • Custom icon hash validation is now enforced for admin-installed PWAs via the WebAppInstallForceList policy, preventing potential script execution from a compromised icon origin. (75d3397)
    BrowserSecurityPolicy
  • A framework for tracking password element coordinates has been implemented, laying the groundwork for future capabilities to redact sensitive information from screenshots. (cf85b4f)
    PasswordManagerPrivacy
  • A security bypass in Exo's remote shell was patched by ensuring activation requests go through the SecurityDelegate. (d8dfdc5)
    ChromeOSSecurity

Android & iOS

  • The ContextMenuUtils.showContextMenu method in components/embedder_support was updated to require a WebContents parameter, a significant signature change for Android embedders using this utility. (e23d6db)
    AndroidUI
  • On Android, MVC components have been implemented for the Vertical Tabs feature. (6ed4816)
    AndroidUITabs
  • On iOS, the ZeroStateSuggestionsService was renamed to ModelLedSuggestionsService in a large refactoring to better reflect its purpose. (4c23f9b)
    iOSUI
  • A vulnerability in Android's Ephemeral Tab feature was fixed by ensuring it uses the last-committed URL, preventing potential spoofing. (2f8fdaf)
    AndroidSecurityNavigation

Services & Core Components

  • New mojom interfaces have been defined for a dedicated WebNN Compiler process as part of a larger effort to move machine learning graph compilation out of the GPU process. (a1a5ab0)
    AIGPUBlink
  • RemotingBridge was refactored out of CastRemotingConnector into its own reusable class. This prepares for Multimedia Redirection, which requires multiple simultaneous connections. (4f44016)
    MediaNetwork
  • New histograms were added to track WebSocket-over-HTTP/3 session reuse and error codes, improving observability of modern networking protocols. (a5a3f02) (6136cd9)
    Network
  • The PIN login backend on ChromeOS (PinBackend, PinSaltStorageImpl) underwent a series of refactorings to improve initialization and dependency injection. (fe04587) (9538cb3) (3e882be)
    ChromeOSAshSecurity

Infrastructure & Tooling

  • A new clang plugin has been implemented to enforce strict dependencies, ensuring that build targets explicitly depend on all headers they include. (79c86cb)
    BuildInfra
  • Several major third-party dependencies were rolled forward, including ffmpeg, libvpx, libaom, and the Expat XML parser. (f170288) (ef26acc) (426fda9) (4741a49)
    InfraMediaBuild
  • Several stale feature flags and their associated code were removed, including AutoDarkMode, OverridePrivacySandboxSettingsLocalTesting, and CacheActivityTaskID. (2708165) (7cb3c20) (2adc0b4)
    Infra

2026-05-20

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-20

Overview

This digest covers 692 commits to the Chromium main branch on May 20th, 2026, with 523 commits identified as relevant to developers. The day was marked by a significant number of breaking changes across C++, Java, and Mojo APIs, particularly in UI, media, and Android tab management. Notable improvements include new asynchronous methods in the Download Manager, performance enhancements on Android, and the introduction of a policy for HTTP header injection. The first and last relevant commits were a285b7adff3d613b3aeff07ff72eb80481fd242a and c354f0a09ec529932d9ed61ad174bc7f303eabaf respectively.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public Android Java classes TabGroupModelFilter, SupportsTabModelObserver, and several methods on TabModel and TabModelSelector have been removed as part of a major refactoring. Embedders must migrate to the consolidated TabModel API. (e51e8f4) (561cded)
    AndroidTabsUI
  • ⚠️The signature of media::VideoCaptureDevice::Client::OnIncomingCapturedImage has been changed to include a new natural_size parameter. Implementations of this public C++ interface must be updated. (636ff60)
    Media
  • ⚠️The ConfirmInfoBar class is now an abstract base class. Direct instantiation is no longer possible; embedders must now use the new static factory method ConfirmInfoBar::Create(). (0cda611)
    UIBanner
  • ⚠️A new virtual method, IsCommandIdEnabled, was added to the public ui::TouchEditable interface. Subclasses must implement this method to compile. (b57bb78)
    UIAndroid
  • ⚠️The signature for searchbox.mojom::QueryAutocomplete was changed, adding a cursor_position parameter. All clients of this mojo API must be updated. (1ad530f)
    UI
  • ⚠️The public C++ function autofill::IsMaskedStorageSupported has been removed. Embedders should use more specific inline predicates instead. (017e1aa)
    Autofill
  • ⚠️The signature of blink::mojom::Frame::AddResourceTimingEntryForFailedSubframeNavigation has changed, replacing URLLoaderCompletionStatus with a new SubframeResourceLengths struct. Mojo clients must be updated. (a6a9a9f)
    BlinkNetworkPerformance
  • ⚠️Several public helper functions like chrome::GetIncognitoBrowserCount() have been removed from chrome/browser/ui/browser_finder.h. Embedders must now use GlobalBrowserCollection directly. (686390b)
    BrowserUI
  • ⚠️The initially_focused_view member type in WidgetDelegate::DelegateParams was changed from std::optional<View*> to std::unique_ptr<ViewTracker>, breaking direct struct initialization. (5c153ab)
    UI
  • ⚠️The audio::SoundsManager::Initialize API signature has changed. It now requires an integer resource ID instead of a std::string_view of raw audio data. (2788a58)
    Media
  • ⚠️The ability to start the Chrome Remote Desktop host on Linux via native messaging has been removed, breaking workflows that relied on this functionality. (ce45a5b)
    SecurityLinux
  • ⚠️Enum values in the stable split_tabs::SplitTabLayout API were renamed from kHorizontal/kVertical to kStacked/kSideBySide. (4abe9bf)
    UITabs

Web Platform & Blink

  • The experimental HTMLUserMediaElement API is now restricted to secure contexts, aligning it with other powerful web APIs. (b19dcb3)
    BlinkSecurityMedia
  • The normalization of non-breaking spaces (&nbsp;) to regular spaces during paste operations is now restricted to rich text editing contexts (contenteditable, designMode), preserving them in plaintext inputs. (b4684a1)
    BlinkHTML
  • Parsing support has been implemented for the new scroll-axis-lock CSS property. (91a0fbc)
    BlinkCSS
  • The SelectiveClipboardFormatRead feature, which allows sites to request specific clipboard formats, has been enabled by default. (137220d)
    BlinkUISecurity
  • Text rendering in native UI now correctly honors the VS-15 (U+FE0E) variation selector, ensuring characters can be explicitly rendered as monochrome text instead of color emoji. (4c82c62)
    BlinkFontsUI
  • Behind the kBreakoutBoxExposePageRelativeAudioCaptureTime flag, audio frames from MediaStreamTrackProcessor will now have page-relative timestamps (performance.now()) instead of system-relative time. (c530df1)
    BlinkWebRTCMedia

Android & iOS

  • On Android, DevTools will now be shown in a separate activity, improving the debugging experience. (5be7d36)
    AndroidDevTools
  • On iOS, the visible viewport is now resized to account for the virtual keyboard's height, matching Android's behavior and improving the user experience during text input. (3688f4b)
    iOSUI
  • The LazyBrowserInterfaceBroker feature has been enabled by default on Android to reduce browser memory consumption. (bdccc71)
    AndroidPerformance
  • The Android TabModel Java interface was updated with a new getOrderedMultiSelectedTabIds method to support multi-tab selection. (4c1b656)
    AndroidTabs
  • The memory footprint of the Android toolbar has been reduced by approximately 1MB by optimizing the size of its bitmap. (b3efd11)
    AndroidPerformanceUI

UI & UX

  • On Windows, developers can now exclude specific views::Widget instances from screen capture by setting a native window property, useful for hiding sensitive or ephemeral UI. (7d0d0a7)
    WindowsMediaUI
  • TableView now supports custom text elision behavior, giving developers more control over how truncated text is displayed. (22b207b)
    UI
  • To prevent potential origin spoofing, Progressive Web App (PWA) windows are now forcibly closed upon uninstallation, bypassing any beforeunload handlers. (d47269c)
    UISecurity
  • The Omnibox context menu now features a dedicated submenu for recent and shared tabs, improving organization and discoverability. (0309cb0)
    UITabs

Services, Enterprise & Core Internals

  • Asynchronous versions of GetAllDownloads and GetDownload have been added to the DownloadManager interface to support future startup performance optimizations. Existing synchronous methods are unchanged. (8cbf419)
    DownloadsPerformance
  • A new enterprise policy, HttpHeaderInjection, has been added, allowing administrators to define and inject custom HTTP headers into requests. (a6a8295)
    EnterprisePolicyNetwork
  • A new experimental field, embedderData, has been added to the DevTools Target.TargetInfo protocol type. Embedders can use the new DevToolsManagerDelegate::GetTargetEmbedderData method to attach custom metadata to targets. (5aa804a)
    DevTools
  • The NNetLanguageIdentifier in AXLanguageDetectionManager is now constructed lazily, saving memory for users who do not use the language detection feature. (c354f0a)
    AccessibilityPerformance
  • A behavioral bug in mojo::BinderMap was fixed to correctly honor RuntimeFeature flags, preventing interfaces from being registered when their associated feature is disabled. (227e91a)
    BaseBlink
  • The HTTP cache initialization logic will now detect and delete empty cache directories on startup, fixing a regression and preventing the system from incorrectly identifying a valid cache. (6dff202)
    NetworkStorage

2026-05-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-19

Overview

This digest covers 704 commits on the main branch, with 530 deemed relevant to developers. The changes span from commit (57f1143) to (d96a1c9).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The HTMLElement.scrollParent DOM API has been changed from a readonly attribute to a method scrollParent(). Web content using property access (element.scrollParent) must be updated to use a function call (element.scrollParent()). (f25a370)
    BlinkHTMLCSS
  • ⚠️The FullscreenObserver class has been removed and replaced with a callback-based subscription model on FullscreenController. Code that implemented FullscreenObserver must be updated to use the new RegisterOnFullscreenStateChanged method. (00d519b)
    UIBlink
  • ⚠️The public Blink API WebLocalFrame::RequestNetworkIdleCallback now returns a base::CallbackListSubscription instead of void. Callers must be updated to manage the subscription's lifecycle. (e871704)
    BlinkNetworkPerformance
  • ⚠️The public media::AudioManager methods GetAudioInputDeviceNames() and GetAudioOutputDeviceNames() have changed their return type from void to bool. Embedders with custom AudioManager implementations must update their method signatures. (d1b707c)
    MediaAudio
  • ⚠️Support for Chromecast audio-only builds has been removed. This includes the deletion of the Chromecast mixer service, the is_cast_audio_only GN flag, and the libcast_media_1.0_audio build target. The public CastAudioManager constructor signature has also changed. (4139460) (843f904) (3322631) (65b9183)
    MediaBuild
  • ⚠️The blink::PagePopupClient public interface is now a GarbageCollected class. Embedders who implement this interface must update their classes to inherit from GarbageCollected and adjust memory management. (9da7c48)
    BlinkUI
  • ⚠️On Android, several deprecated public methods such as getCurrentTabGroupModelFilter() have been removed from the TabModelSelector Java interface. Embedders relying on these methods will experience build failures. (c0a50ed)
    AndroidTabs
  • ⚠️The flags field and the ActionFlags enum have been removed from the public ui::AXActionData structure. Embedders using this field in the accessibility API will have their builds broken. (da04fe8)
    AccessibilityUI
  • ⚠️Support for Chrome policies has been completely removed from the headless shell. Automation that relied on configuring headless via policies will no longer function. (fcdf7e8)
    PolicyTesting
  • ⚠️The last_active_time_ticks and last_active_elapsed_text properties have been removed from the tabs_api.mojom.Tab Mojo interface. (5f89e9c)
    TabsUI
  • ⚠️The constructor for media::AudioDiscardHelper no longer accepts a delayed_discard boolean parameter, simplifying the API. Code that constructs this class must be updated. (9877688)
    MediaAudio
  • ⚠️On iOS, the deprecated public function IsDisableU18FeedbackIosEnabled() has been removed from the features header. (e738f4b)
    iOS

Web Platform & Blink

  • Big5 encoding and decoding has been aligned with the WHATWG standard, which may affect sites that relied on the previous non-standard implementation. (846270c)
    BlinkI18n
  • Support was added for closest-corner and farthest-corner keywords in CSS circle() and ellipse() shape functions. (925f513)
    BlinkCSS
  • A shadow DOM leak was fixed where touch events could expose nodes in a closed shadow root via a window event listener. (2cf3934)
    BlinkSecurityHTML
  • The Language Model Prompt API's PromptBuilder was hardened to prevent renderer crashes from faulty or cross-origin tainted image data. (354ee2d)
    AIBlinkSecurity
  • The paint order for replaced normal-flow stacking contexts like <video> was fixed to ensure they render inline with sibling content correctly. (b629d9b)
    BlinkCompositorCSS
  • A performance optimization in SVGGeometryElement now lazily allocates path_length_ to improve scores on benchmarks like Speedometer3. (4548792)
    BlinkPerformance
  • Creation of AudioDestination in Web Audio can now fail gracefully under memory pressure, preventing a hard crash from out-of-memory conditions. (c5401c1)
    BlinkMediaAudio

Security

  • On Windows, PWA installation was hardened to sanitize app names that are reserved device names (e.g., COM1), preventing potential DoS or unauthorized device I/O. (1981552)
    SecurityWindowsUI
  • A keyjacking risk in ui::DialogModel was mitigated by adding an option to block key events during the initial input protection period. This was enabled for the Digital Identity interstitial. (e64e0f3)
    SecurityUI
  • On macOS, the app-shim process now correctly reconfigures PartitionAlloc after startup, enabling critical security mitigations like MiraclePtr in this unsandboxed process. (68319a1)
    SecuritymacOSBase
  • The gpu::gles2::Logger class no longer leaks its heap address in GL error logs, removing a deterministic ASLR bypass for the GPU process. (a4438e3)
    SecurityGPUWebGL
  • A TOCTOU vulnerability in Link-to-Text was patched by ensuring pending selector requests are canceled on all cross-document navigations, preventing URL/title leakage. (03a83a4)
    SecurityNavigation
  • A site isolation bypass in Media Foundation CDM storage was fixed by enforcing correct ACLs on existing CDM store roots. (4e5395b)
    SecurityMediaWindows
  • New interfaces were added to support hardware-backed attestation keys for Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC). (17ddc0a) (66beec8)
    SecurityCrypto

UI & UX

  • A new searchable target language selection view was implemented for the Translate bubble, replacing the legacy combobox to better handle a large number of languages. (c03576a)
    UII18n
  • The CustomizeChrome feature was enabled by default. (b9adff3)
    UIThemes
  • A safer, unique_ptr-based CreateBubble method was introduced for creating views::BubbleDialogDelegate widgets, and older methods were deprecated. (0b4629d)
    UIBase
  • A bug in the Omnibox was fixed where about:blank would incorrectly be shown for slow-loading new tabs, ensuring the pending destination URL is displayed instead. (bd632b9)
    UIBrowser

Android

  • The stylus handwriting feature, which offloads probing to a background thread, was enabled by default. (f0c947a)
    AndroidUI
  • The maximum number of audio output streams was increased from 10 to 50 for desktop mode, preventing errors when many tabs are open. (f94ef17)
    AndroidMediaAudio
  • The nested app menu was updated to include items like "Name window", "QR code", "Copy link", and a new "Passwords and autofill" parent item. (196652b) (03b82c4) (f893716)
    AndroidUI
  • The PDF viewer now has a toggle to switch between fit-to-page-width and fit-to-page-height. (a935e98)
    AndroidPDFUI

iOS

  • A security vulnerability was patched by validating canonical URL schemes to prevent URL spoofing in the Share Sheet. (96337b3)
    iOSSecurityUI
  • A fix prevents stale file-choosing metadata from persisting across navigations, addressing a potential UI spoofing vector. (e0130db)
    iOSSecurityUI

Build & Infrastructure

  • A major refactoring of //chrome/browser/BUILD.gn was performed to break down the monolithic :browser target into many smaller source_set targets. This improves build modularity and may require updates to custom build configurations for embedders. (a3e448d)
    BuildInfra
  • The libafl fuzzing library and its dependencies have been added to the Chromium tree to support fuzzing efforts. (ee4017c) (b1cafcd)
    BuildTestingSecurity
  • The build process for Android was updated to use R8's resource shrinker, replacing the custom Python scripts for removing unused resources. (0e0e890)
    BuildAndroidInfra
  • The Windows updater was refactored to remove its dependency on the aging ATL/WTL frameworks in favor of standard Chromium primitives. (0806fe8)
    UpdaterWindowsBuild
  • A presubmit check was added for Cargo.lock to ensure it remains valid and up-to-date, improving build determinism for Rust components. (d685f19)
    BuildInfra

Extensions

  • Old code for upgrading extensions from the legacy New Tab Page permission to chrome_url_overrides was removed. Extensions still using the old permission will now be disabled for a permission increase. (81cc594)
    Extensions
  • The default Content Security Policy (CSP) for MV3+ extensions is now also applied to extensions of type kUserScript for greater consistency. (6de2ae4)
    ExtensionsSecurity

2026-05-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-18

Overview

On a busy day with 568 total commits (392 relevant), the main branch saw a significant number of breaking API changes across core browser, sync, and media components. Major refactoring efforts continue, alongside security hardening and the ongoing 'GlowUp' UI refresh. The first and last relevant commits were (de2beb1) and (f48d543).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️Over 20 enterprise policies have been removed, including AppLaunchAutomation and LegacySameSiteCookieBehaviorEnabled. Administrative scripts and configurations relying on these policies will no longer function. (bf54c2e) (94ea7ec)
    PolicyEnterpriseChromeOS
  • ⚠️Widely-used browser lookup helper functions in chrome/browser/ui/browser_finder.h (e.g., FindTabbedBrowser, FindBrowserWithProfile) have been removed. Developers must migrate to the new ProfileBrowserCollection and GlobalBrowserCollection APIs. (1d867aa)
    BrowserUITabs
  • ⚠️The public WebContentsDelegate::OnDidBlockNavigation method signature was changed to include initiator_url and initiator_origin. Embedders implementing this delegate must update their code to match the new signature. (64a041d)
    NavigationBrowser
  • ⚠️The public SyncService interface has been modified, removing the deprecated IsSyncFeatureEnabled() and IsSyncFeatureActive() methods. The behavior of GetSelectedOsTypes() has also changed, which may impact embedder logic. (9efc428)
    Sync
  • ⚠️The core MojomParse trait in Rust Mojo bindings now requires a generic Context parameter, breaking all existing implementations. Code must be updated to use new conversion methods instead of standard From/Into traits. (bf955d0) (4fb6547)
    BaseNetwork
  • ⚠️The public Java OmniboxActionFactory on Android was refactored from an interface to a class with static factory methods. Downstream projects using the previous factory interface must update their code. (7018851)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️The ExtensionKeybindingRegistry::Delegate class has been removed and the ExtensionKeybindingRegistry constructor signature has changed, breaking integrations that manage extension keyboard shortcuts. (f08221c)
    ExtensionsUI
  • ⚠️The return type of WASAPIAudioOutputStream::RenderAudioFromSource on Windows was changed from bool to HRESULT, breaking binary compatibility for consumers of this public media API. (a43cf79)
    MediaWindows
  • ⚠️A dependency roll for HarfBuzz now requires a minimum FreeType version of 2.8.0. This is a breaking change for build systems that may be using an older version of FreeType. (8c34f88)
    BuildFonts
  • ⚠️The public BrowserFocusController::Delegate class has been removed as part of a refactor, breaking embedders of //chrome who may have been using or subclassing this delegate. (64c1527)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️The public API setRendererLibraryPrefetchMode has been removed from Android WebView's AwContentsStatics and the support library, breaking any embedder using it. (f4dcadf)
    AndroidPerformance
  • ⚠️The SharingService API was refactored, renaming the public method SendUnencryptedMessageToDevice to SendIosPushMessageToDevice and removing the underlying delegate mechanism. (cf82324)
    BrowseriOS

Security

  • A Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) race condition in speech recognition was fixed, which could have allowed an extension to bypass audio capture permissions. (0086720)
    SecurityExtensionsMedia
  • The password manager's check for cross-origin iframes was improved to validate the entire ancestor chain, preventing potential password leakage in complex 'sandwich' (A-B-A) iframe scenarios. (b80505d)
    PasswordManagerSecurity
  • The Android WebShare API now validates that shared URLs use HTTP or HTTPS, and will terminate a renderer that attempts to pass an invalid scheme. (38749a0)
    SecurityAndroidBlink
  • Validation for ICC color profiles fetched from the X11 root window was hardened to prevent potential memory exhaustion and integer overflows. (826fa20)
    SecurityLinuxGPU
  • An ASLR bypass vulnerability was fixed by replacing raw pointer logging with opaque tokens in the audio service. (552949a)
    SecurityMedia

Web Platform & Blink

  • A new syntax for line-clamp has been implemented: max-lines: <integer> auto. This allows clamping by both line count and height, improving web compatibility. (7eec7df)
    BlinkCSS
  • Print requests originating from fenced frames are now rejected. A compromised renderer attempting to print from a fenced frame will be terminated. (07e3684)
    BlinkPrintingSecurity
  • The runtime flag SvgTransformOnNestedSvgElement has been removed, making the feature permanently enabled and allowing transform attributes on nested <svg> elements. (c4963ac)
    BlinkHTML

UI & UX

  • The "GlowUp" UI refresh continues with new rounded icons and themed colors across many surfaces, including Downloads, Extensions, Certificate Manager, and the Message Center. (08cd8e0) (50404b4) (1db29e1) (227aeee)
    UIThemes
  • The Omnibox context menu has been simplified by removing several items, including emoji, writing direction, and autofill options, as part of the 'GlowUp' refresh. (e9141f4)
    UI
  • A new "Lock Screen" trigger was implemented for the Smart Restart feature, which can automatically restart the browser for pending updates when the screen is locked. (188f353)
    UpdaterUI
  • A bug was fixed where holding the Esc key would not exit fullscreen mode if focus was on a native UI element like the Omnibox. (92368d9)
    UIBrowser

Android

  • Windowed Incognito mode is now enabled by default on large-screen Android devices. (e57ef26)
    AndroidUIPrivacy
  • The fontSettings.getFontList() extension API on desktop Android was updated to return a list of generic font families (e.g., serif, sans-serif) to provide compatibility, as Android lacks a system API to enumerate all installed fonts. (a9f439b)
    AndroidExtensionsFonts
  • A prompt to upgrade from approximate to precise location is being introduced for the Android Geolocation API. (b50ce6e)
    AndroidPrivacy
  • A rendering issue with zero-copy camera frames using Dawn/Graphite was fixed, which previously caused swapped U and V planes (pink/green colors). (e153883)
    AndroidMediaGPU

Sync & Accounts

  • A crash caused by corrupted local Nigori (encryption) data is now prevented by validating the data on startup and allowing for a safe reset. (0ab182f)
    SyncCrypto
  • The process for setting up a new custom passphrase was refactored to move key derivation into the SyncEngine, simplifying the API surface. (fc62658)
    SyncCrypto

Build & Infrastructure

  • A new performance bot, android-pixel10_webview-perf, was added to CI and Pinpoint configurations to run WebView performance tests. (3cb8f70)
    InfraTestingAndroid
  • The Rust toolchain was repackaged and several third-party Rust crates were updated. (748a3f2) (362b960)
    BuildInfra

2026-05-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-17

Overview

This digest analyzes 7 commits, identifying one major breaking change. The most significant update involves stricter URI validation in Android's SelectFileDialog, which may impact embedders. Other changes include security hardening for the Wayland server, test infrastructure improvements, and minor UI and localization updates.

Android

  • ⚠️The SelectFileDialog public API now rejects scheme-less URIs and any URI scheme other than content://. This is a security hardening measure but is a breaking change for embedders who may have been passing other valid URI types, such as those with a file:// scheme, which will now fail. (a652043)
    AndroidUISecurity

Security

  • A potential buffer overflow in the Wayland server implementation was fixed by replacing a raw C-style array with std::array for improved memory safety. (16cb4d0)
    SecurityLinuxCompositor

Testing

  • Test suites for Sync and Signin utilities have been refactored to remove dependencies on the heavy-weight BrowserWithTestWindowTest fixture, improving test performance and reducing complexity. (d018942) (6ca077d)
    TestingSyncProfiles
  • The internal WaitTool for automated testing now enforces a URL check for tabs it operates on. (5b8b5c3)
    TestingInfra

UI

  • A plural string for "Sharing n tabs" has been added to the contextual tasks and compose box UI to ensure the counter is displayed correctly for multiple tabs. (3f982b3)
    UI

Internationalization

  • Localization string files (.xtb) were updated across various components and platforms to align with the latest source strings. (ca327f2)
    I18n

2026-05-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-16

Overview

This digest covers 58 commits from the Chromium repository. The analysis focuses on changes impacting GPU, security, the web platform, UI, and internal refactoring. One major breaking change was identified in the GPU control list API, alongside several security hardening improvements and new feature implementations for the Web Speech API, PDF annotations, and canvas rendering.

GPU

  • ⚠️The GpuControlList::MakeDecision() method signature has been changed by removing the default value for the ignored_entries parameter. Embedders calling this public API method without explicitly providing this argument will now encounter compile-time errors. (c7427fc)
    GPUBase
  • Introduces more rigorous validation for plane metadata when populating VADRMPRIMESurfaceDescriptor in the VaapiWrapper. This includes verifying plane pitch and ensuring the plane's memory range is within the DMA-buf bounds, hardening the component against invalid data. (69d10d8)
    GPUMedia
  • The IOSurfaceIsWebGPUCompatible() helper function has been moved from //media to //ui/gfx/mac to allow its use from //gpu without creating a circular dependency. This is an internal refactoring to support upcoming changes in MappableBuffer. (59ad18d)
    GPUMediamacOS

Security

  • Hardens the WebUSB implementation on macOS against a Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerability related to endpoint aliasing. The fix rejects claiming an interface if it advertises a duplicate endpoint address already mapped to a different interface, aligning Chromium's logic with libusb's behavior. (a74fd42)
    USBSecuritymacOS
  • Prevents potential shortcut hijacking in the XDG Global Shortcuts portal on Linux. Suggested accelerators for manifest-defined commands are now cleared if they conflict with reserved browser shortcuts (e.g., Ctrl+T), mitigating abuse while still allowing users to manually assign them. (39567c3)
    ExtensionsSecurityLinux
  • Fixes a potential Use-After-Free in the Linux printing portal (PrintDialogLinuxPortal) by replacing a raw aura::Window pointer with an aura::WindowTracker. This ensures the window's validity is checked after an asynchronous D-Bus call, preventing memory corruption if the window is destroyed during the operation. (2286275)
    PrintingSecurityLinux
  • Adds a bounds check to HidConnectionWin on Windows to harden against a potential buffer read overflow. This ensures that when stripping a leading zero byte from a feature report, the bytes_transferred count is validated against the buffer size. (0bac4a7)
    SecurityWindowsBrowser

Web Platform

  • Improves the performance of the Web Speech API by removing an artificial 2-second audio buffering delay in the on-device speech recognition engine. Audio chunks are now sent to the ASR stream immediately, significantly reducing the time-to-first-token for streaming dictation. (e31bc6d)
    BlinkPerformanceAI
  • Implements support for rendering <video> elements within a <canvas> using drawElement. The implementation synchronously reads video frame pixels and stores them in a pixel-backed image in the display list, enabling canvas pixel readback operations from a worker thread. (1d44c9d)
    BlinkHTMLMedia
  • Fixes a bug in WebCrypto CryptoKey serialization for the new ML-KEM and ML-DSA post-quantum algorithms. A new serialization tag was introduced to handle algorithms without key parameters, and new WPT tests were added to prevent future regressions. (23479ab) (dc989b8)
    BlinkCryptoSecurity

UI & UX

  • The audio processor for voice search has been significantly improved. It now uses a band-limited Root Mean Square (RMS) to specifically target human speech frequencies, incorporates adaptive noise tracking for better SNR, and applies dynamic cubic Bezier shaping to normalize audio levels. This results in a more responsive and accurate UI visualization for voice input. (43cbbf2)
    UIAI
  • On desktop, Omnibox suggestions are no longer pre-selected in a zero-prefix context (when the omnibox is empty). The first suggestion will only be pre-selected after the user starts typing. (7a891d0)
    UIBrowser
  • Improves native UI font rendering by correctly interpreting the emoji presentation selector (VS-16, U+FE0F). Text-default characters like ©, ®, and ™ followed by VS-16 will now correctly render using the platform's color emoji font in surfaces like tab titles, matching the behavior in web content. (1c45c78)
    UIFonts

PDF

  • Implements editing, deleting, and undo/redo for text annotations within the PDF Ink Signatures feature. This involves syncing state between the frontend and the PDFium backend, managing FPDF_PAGEOBJECT handles, and adding new client methods to discard and update text objects. (f05ce42) (b1809ed) (04cd755) (4f6437d) (feed479) (f880d93) (f469ee2)
    PDFUI

Android

  • The WebUI-based compose box is now enabled on Android (for desktop-class devices), bringing the feature to a new platform. The initial implementation excludes Lens features and the onboarding tooltip. (060cd97)
    AndroidUIAI
  • Introduces UI in Glic Settings on Android to indicate when the feature is managed by enterprise policy. (aad9316)
    AndroidEnterprisePolicy

Internals & Refactoring

  • The WebAppLaunchProcess has been refactored to use the central browser_navigator for all navigation paths. This consolidates navigation logic and simplifies the web app launch flow. (fe7265c)
    BrowserUI
  • The JSON structure of the chrome://web-app-internals debugging page has been normalized. The data is now a flat dictionary instead of an array of single-key objects, which allows for stronger typing and easier data access in the frontend. (f058bd2)
    DevToolsBrowser

2026-05-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-15

Overview

This summary covers 729 commits on the main branch, with 555 deemed relevant to developers. The changes span from May 15, 2026, starting with commit 5dae0b1af587f8b08c72b7ffa9b5acd5905cebb5 and ending with f98f0f4e339caeadfee03ffacdb1f1473b75b38c. The day was marked by an exceptionally high number of breaking changes across UI, core types, media, and platform-specific APIs, requiring significant attention from embedders.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public API for customizing colors on views::Textfield has been changed. Methods like SetBackgroundColor and SetTextColor which accepted an SkColor have been removed. Developers must now use the new methods that accept a std::optional<ui::ColorId>, such as SetTextColorId and SetSelectionBackgroundColorId. (b44a41b) (f20aaa7) (f262b03)
    UI
  • ⚠️Several core types and UI classes have been refactored. The RTTI class ui::FrameworkSpecificImplementation is now ui::SafeCastable (727bd8f7). The base::LanguageCode class no longer has a constexpr constructor to support variants (8805c200). The sql::DatabaseOptions::set_no_sync_on_wal_mode() method has been renamed to set_no_sync() (60871903). (727bd8f) (8805c20) (6087190)
    UIBaseStorage
  • ⚠️Two significant behavioral changes affect web applications. The engine now enforces stricter, spec-compliant parsing for ARIA roles, ignoring roles like listitem if they lack the required parent context (09e48bc9). Separately, WebUSB security has been hardened to block standard control transfers (e.g., SET_CONFIGURATION) via the generic controlTransfer method, requiring the use of dedicated WebIDL methods instead (3169e981). (09e48bc) (3169e98)
    BlinkAccessibilityUSB
  • ⚠️As part of a large-scale refactoring, public methods related to PWA display modes have been renamed from *Borderless* to *Unframed* (e.g., AppUsesBorderlessMode is now AppUsesUnframedMode) (5eb2a986). Additionally, the public method Browser::IsPointerLocked() has been removed; callers must now use the ExclusiveAccessManager (760506d2). (5eb2a98) (760506d)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️The media and graphics pipelines saw several breaking API changes. In viz, content_bounds was removed from OutputSurfaceFrame and the OverlayProcessorInterface::ProcessForOverlays signature was changed (bf710c23). The return type of media::AudioDecoderConfig::target_output_channel_layout() is now const ChannelLayoutConfig& (9a0e8b3a). The audio::SoundsManager singleton has been replaced by audio::GlobalSoundsManager (c2530960). (bf710c2) (9a0e8b3) (c253096)
    MediaCompositorGPU
  • ⚠️Multiple platform-specific APIs were changed. On Android, MediaRouteProvider::startObservingMediaSinks now requires an origin parameter (e66d0b06), and AutocompleteController::Stop now takes an AutocompleteStopReason enum (134822b9). On iOS, PaymentsAutofillClient::OnCardDataAvailable now requires an origin (73be2527). For ChromeOS, core timezone utilities were moved and their signatures changed (16c1f71f, d6b4bd51). (e66d0b0) (134822b) (73be252) (16c1f71) (d6b4bd5)
    AndroidiOSChromeOS
  • ⚠️Several service-level APIs were broken. The public method AutocompleteController::UpdateMatchDestinationURLWithInvocationSource was removed (99def36f). For Sync, SyncServiceCrypto::SetExplicitPassphraseDecryptionNigoriKey() (7542bc21) and SendTabToSelfModelObserver::SendTabToSelfModelLoaded() (7e872ba1) were removed. For networking, content::PrefetchUpdateHeadersParams was moved and renamed to network::HttpRequestHeadersUpdateParams (a89eecdf). (99def36) (7542bc2) (7e872ba) (a89eecd)
    AutofillSyncNetwork
  • ⚠️The enable_video_capture_service GN build argument has been removed, which may break Chromecast build configurations (3e0a630d). For external management servers, the EXTENSION_UPDATE_CHECK enum in device_management_backend.proto was renamed to BROWSER_EXTENSION_UPDATE_CHECK (ce1de7f6). For ChromeOS extensions, the searchSuggestEnabled property was removed from the file_manager_private API (a9bfde92). (3e0a630) (ce1de7f) (a9bfde9)
    BuildEnterpriseChromeOS

Security

  • Protections were added to disallow message connections from sandboxed extension pages (885bc095). Storage access levels are now correctly enforced for onChanged events originating from Chrome Sync or enterprise policy (19a50035). (885bc09) (19a5003)
    ExtensionsSecuritySync
  • Several vulnerabilities were addressed, including a fix for a compromised iframe injecting malicious nodes into a popup (5b5a413e), a potential data race in GPU D3D image backing (a0683f84), and a Use-After-Free in the Private State Token (PST) issuance helper (67439edd). The Web Bluetooth implementation was also updated to correctly check the blocklist for a descriptor's parent characteristic (a0b55d79). (5b5a413) (a0683f8) (67439ed) (a0b55d7)
    SecurityGPUBluetooth

Web Platform & APIs

  • The OpenH264 software encoder is now enabled by default on Android, providing a fallback for WebRTC, WebCodecs, and MediaRecorder (a4f5a3bd). In WebAudio, DirectConvolver and resamplers were updated to work with any input size, improving flexibility (76217b20). Additionally, the H264 decoder was optimized to prevent timeouts from large frame_num gaps in corrupted streams (c0cb19c6). (a4f5a3b) (76217b2) (c0cb19c)
    MediaWebRTCAndroid
  • The HTML Canvas drawElement method now throws an exception if an Element or ElementImage is drawn into a different canvas than the one it originated from. This improves consistency and security by preventing cross-canvas data leakage. (dbe9c63)
    BlinkHTMLSecurity
  • The ModulePreloadReferrer feature is now enabled by default (183f3e98). A resource leak in the Web Speech API when using a MediaStreamTrack has been fixed (b676377a). For WebXR, GL resource cleanup has been moved to pre-finalizers to prevent use-after-free errors during garbage collection (85f3563f). (183f3e9) (b676377) (85f3563)
    BlinkMediaXR

UI & UX

  • UI performance has been improved by preventing re-entrancy in browser layout and caching dialog anchor values to reduce redundant layout passes (4274c9f7, 19d5f27c). For GPU-accelerated rendering, the CompositorFrameSink is now created earlier for new renderers, reducing a browser-renderer roundtrip and improving responsiveness (227fa3e4). (4274c9f) (19d5f27) (227fa3e)
    PerformanceUICompositor
  • The Composebox and Omnibox components received numerous updates, including fixing IME interactions (7d6174f0), improving keyboard accessibility (d83072a5), and creating a dedicated ntp-composebox fork for the New Tab Page (3ddf82a4, 6466d1df). (7d6174f) (d83072a) (3ddf82a) (6466d1d)
    UIAI
  • The MenuModel API was enhanced to allow placing a minor icon to the right of minor text, enabling richer menu item displays (5fff9228). The download bubble UI has been further decoupled from BrowserView by using BrowserWindowInterface in its public API (371912c3). (5fff922) (371912c)
    UIDownloads

Platform Specific (Android, iOS, ChromeOS)

  • On Android, the kAndroidSmsOtpFilling feature for Autofill was enabled by default (67f85739). Startup performance is improved by deferring the creation of ContentView wrappers for frozen background tabs (3cbee391). Numerous UI refactorings occurred, including centralizing button management in the bottom bar (dc80a657) and implementing the UI layout for the Autofill 'Flyout Sheet' (e9b87bb9). (67f8573) (3cbee39) (dc80a65) (e9b87bb)
    AndroidAutofillPerformance
  • The iOS crash handler was fixed to prevent re-entry into partition alloc by caching a feature flag value at startup (5e528541). A bug in the AppBar's In-Product Help (IPH) was fixed by ensuring the presenter is retained until dismissed (d331699c). For builds using Blink, Perfetto tracing is now handled by the content layer instead of the legacy IOSTracingController (28a7ac5a). (5e52854) (d331699) (28a7ac5)
    iOSInfra

2026-05-14

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-14

Overview

On 2026-05-14, the main branch saw 597 commits, with 445 deemed relevant for this summary. This digest covers changes from commit 5e8bd89900b8 to 3eec1c723b47. The day was marked by a high number of significant breaking changes affecting core APIs like media::VideoFrame, PartitionAlloc, URLLoaderThrottle, and various Android and ChromeOS interfaces. Other notable updates include the stabilization of the CSS text-fit property, multiple security hardening improvements, and continued refactoring of Ash and iOS UI components.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The ycbcr_info() and set_ycbcr_info() methods have been removed from the core media::VideoFrame class. This information is now stored in media::VideoFrameMetadata. This is a major break for any code directly interacting with YCbCr info on video frames. (55b4406)
    Media
  • ⚠️Core PartitionAlloc headers (e.g., partition_root.h, partition_page.h) have been moved into an internal/ subdirectory. Embedders directly including these headers by their old paths will experience build failures. (7c766eb)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️The public API signature of blink::URLLoaderThrottle::BeforeWillRedirectRequest has changed. It removes three parameters and changes the net::RedirectInfo parameter from a pointer to a const reference. All custom URLLoaderThrottle implementations must be updated. (8c41713)
    BlinkNetwork
  • ⚠️The Android build configuration for resource optimization has been changed. Granular GN arguments (strip_resource_names, strip_unused_resources, etc.) have been removed and consolidated into a single optimize_resources argument, breaking build systems that relied on the old flags. (bc1ff55)
    AndroidBuild
  • ⚠️On Android, the getFilter() method has been removed from the public TabModelDelegate interface as part of a larger refactoring. Embedders who implement or call methods on TabModelDelegate will have build breaks. (73cd922)
    AndroidTabs
  • ⚠️The public Android SideUiObserver interface has been refactored. The onSideUiSpecsChanged() method's behavior was altered, and two new methods, onTransitionBegun() and onTransitionEnded(), were introduced. Implementers must update their code to handle the new interface. (c4e9467)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️The CancelPrintJob method has been removed from the public crosapi::mojom::LocalPrinter interface. Clients must now use the Ash-specific ash::printing::print_management::PrintingManager API directly. (aca3120)
    ChromeOSPrinting
  • ⚠️The public variations::VariationsServiceClient interface was refactored. Two virtual methods were removed and replaced by a new GetAllProfilesKeys virtual method, requiring embedders to update their implementations. (07c8aa0)
    BrowserInfra
  • ⚠️The Chromecast enable_audio_capture_service build flag and its entire associated subsystem, including the CastAudioInputStream class, have been deleted. Similarly, the enable_perfetto_tracing flag and its perfetto_tracing_client were also removed. (64cab58) (58ad4ce)
    BuildMediaPerformance

Security

  • A vulnerability in Android WebView was fixed where a compromised renderer could bypass the setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(false) policy. The browser process now correctly determines the site_for_cookies. (672e778)
    AndroidSecurityNetwork
  • A potential Site Isolation bypass was fixed. MHTML subframes are now forced to commit with opaque origins to prevent a compromised renderer from spoofing a subframe's origin. (e4b92a6)
    SecurityBlink
  • The Windows updater was hardened with stricter temporary directory selection, sanitized path validation to block directory traversal, and improved bounds controls on the COM RPC layer. (58d2139)
    UpdaterSecurityWindows
  • To prevent data corruption from a compromised renderer, the browser process will now terminate a renderer that supplies invalid index IDs or keys to IndexedDB. (206286e)
    StorageSecurityBlink
  • On ChromeOS, user-imported ONC files can no longer silently establish web trust for root CAs, as TrustBits are now stripped from such configurations. (c0dd46e) (1db6c27)
    ChromeOSSecurityPolicy

Blink & Web Platform

  • The text-fit CSS property has been enabled by default and is now considered stable. Follow-up commits fixed related bugs with line height, text emphasis, and underline offset scaling. (5294214) (98c96b6) (9de481f) (37463c7)
    BlinkCSS
  • The chain value for the overscroll-behavior CSS property has been shipped, allowing web developers to explicitly control scroll chaining behavior. (424d2b9)
    BlinkCSS
  • Initial infrastructure for the Declarative Performance Observer feature was landed, including a header parser and a skeleton PageLoadMetricsObserver. (2d4c09d) (de01842)
    PerformanceBlink
  • A race condition that could lead to premature Service Worker termination has been fixed by introducing a keepalive sequence number. (d92cc43)
    ServiceWorkerBlink
  • To enhance privacy, active sensor connections are now severed immediately when a user revokes the "Sensors" permission for a frame, preventing further data leakage. (19452b0)
    PrivacyBlinkSecurity

Android

  • A bug causing an Application Not Responding (ANR) error when reading archived tab groups was fixed by optimizing the count retrieval logic. (832219f)
    AndroidUITabs
  • A fix was implemented to ensure WebXR sessions always render in fullscreen, addressing a UI "squishing" issue caused by system insets on newer Android versions. (399a6d7)
    XRAndroid
  • The ExtensionURLLoaderThrottle is now correctly enabled on desktop Android, and availability for "controlled frame" APIs has been properly configured for the platform. (accec1e) (46c775e)
    ExtensionsAndroid

ChromeOS & Ash

  • The ongoing effort to decouple Ash from the browser continues, with DateTimeSection, InternetHandler, FingerprintHandler, and FilesSection all migrated to use lower-level Ash APIs instead of ProfileHelper. (4d70581) (a264ff9) (47991d8) (3d2cde3)
    AshChromeOSUI
  • As part of the "GlowUp" UI refresh, a large number of vector icons in ash/ and components/ were renamed to prepare for new icon assets. (c75b4a7) (4a3af8f)
    AshUIThemes
  • In the Camera App (CCA), the cloud save warning dialog was migrated from Polymer's cr-elements to Lit to prevent touch event interception issues. (4918888)
    ChromeOSUI

iOS

  • Significant refactoring unified Gemini/AI entry points, routing the app bar's "Ask" button and the Page Action Menu through a generalized coordinator. (41840d4) (689f458) (c38cd1f)
    iOSUIAI
  • A long-press context menu was implemented for the Tab Grid button on iPad, which now also includes a tab count label. (634615e) (61e469f)
    iOSUITabs
  • The WebAuthnCredentialsDelegate was integrated into the manual fill mediator to enable fetching and displaying passkeys in the manual fallback UI. (e22b581)
    iOSPasswordManagerSecurity
  • Async trace events were added to track the duration of JavaScript execution tasks dispatched to WebKit, aiding in performance analysis on iOS. (a30c87f)
    iOSDevToolsPerformance

Build System & Infrastructure

  • Support was added for Visual Studio 2026, and several third-party libraries were rolled, including libvpx, libaom, ICU, and Rust crates. (1550583) (516d005) (85e3a1d) (36157b4)
    BuildInfra
  • Several obsolete feature flags that have been enabled by default were removed, including kPrefetchFixHeaderUpdatesOnRedirect, kFastPairHandshakeLongTermRefactor, and SubframeImportance. (5b9e0a9) (9eb4f10) (f883619)
    InfraBuild
  • The BackgroundTracingManager was moved from content/ to services/ to allow its use in environments without the full content layer, such as on iOS. (0be4281)
    PerformanceInfra

Performance & Memory

  • The migration from base::MemoryPressureListener to the more flexible base::MemoryConsumer API continues, with BlobMemoryController and SlopBucket being updated to use the new system. (cd4a600) (59d3eb3) (3eec1c7)
    PerformanceBase
  • The concepts of DeviceScaleFactor and RenderedDeviceScaleFactor in LayerTreeHostImpl were unified, simplifying scale factor handling in the compositor. (c923203)
    CompositorPerformance

2026-05-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-13

Overview

On May 13, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 682 commits, with 499 identified as relevant to developers. This summary highlights major API changes, security enhancements, and performance improvements. The day's activity started with commit 5b6de9caa4cd00624e25a4dea1a7adcf9eea2e70 and concluded with 010fba64a502640c492a2d53d47613cbe5881ae6.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The <webview> tag and chrome.webviewTag API have been removed from Chrome Apps on Windows, Mac, and Linux. This feature is now restricted to ChromeOS only, breaking a stable and widely-used API on desktop platforms. (dfa28c4)
    ExtensionsBrowser
  • ⚠️Several PartitionAlloc headers have been moved into an internal/ subdirectory. This is a public API change that will break any build that directly includes headers like partition_page.h or partition_root.h from their old location. (cd836a8)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️The core compositor class cc::LayerTreeMutatorClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeMutatorDelegate, and its SetClient method is now SetDelegate. This will break embedders who implement or use this animation interface. (34492d7)
    CompositorBlink
  • ⚠️The public SendTabToSelfModelObserver C++ interface has been significantly changed. All method names now require an On prefix (e.g., EntriesAddedRemotely is now OnEntriesAddedRemotely), and the OnSendTabToSelfModelLoaded method has been removed entirely, breaking all existing observer implementations. (35d38a6) (2ca3334)
    SyncBrowser
  • ⚠️The remoting::AudioInjector C++ interface was refactored. It no longer inherits from protocol::AudioStub, and the InjectAudioPacket() method was replaced by a new pure virtual method SetSampleInfo(). Custom audio injection implementations will need to be updated. (5259bac)
    MediaWebRTC
  • ⚠️On Android, the public Java interface ChromeAndroidTaskFeature has changed. The onAddedToTask(long) method was removed and onAddedToTask(InitInfo) is now a mandatory method, which will cause compilation errors for any class implementing this interface. (5af4b45)
    Android
  • ⚠️The PasswordStoreBackend::InitBackend method signature has been changed, removing the AffiliatedMatchHelper* parameter. This will break any custom PasswordStoreBackend implementations. (4fdbebe)
    PasswordManagerStorage
  • ⚠️The public class AccessibilityAnnotationSyncBridge and its usages within SyncServiceFactory have been completely removed. Embedders using this sync data type will be broken. (bc57ea3)
    SyncAccessibility
  • ⚠️On ChromeOS, the GetPolicies method has been removed from the crosapi::mojom::LocalPrinter interface, breaking clients that use it to fetch printing policies. (28102da)
    ChromeOSPrinting

Security & Privacy

  • The PartitionAllocWithAdvancedChecks (MiracleObject) memory safety feature has been enabled by default for the Browser UI process, helping to detect and prevent Use-After-Free vulnerabilities. (34c2ad4)
    SecurityPerformanceBase
  • A site isolation bypass in the Web Serial API for non-default StoragePartitions (e.g., in guest views) has been fixed by blocking access for HTTP/HTTPS origins in those partitions. (16e3c60)
