  • A check for invalid navigation headers sent by a renderer process was upgraded from a DumpWithoutCrashing to a full renderer process kill, hardening the browser against misbehaving renderers. (e0a0f3e)
  • A use-after-free vulnerability in the macOS implementation of FilePathWatcherFSEvents was fixed by introducing a ref-counted context. (cb6f7a4)
  • A potential local privilege escalation vulnerability in Chrome Remote Desktop on Windows was fixed by preventing the service from following reparse points when writing configuration files. (f67fb61)
  • A sanitization bypass in FrameSerializer was fixed. Mixed-case scripting attributes (e.g., ONLOAD) in MHTML files could previously lead to script execution on re-opening. (19cb047)
  • Security for the Service Worker Static Routing API was strengthened to prevent a Cross-Origin Resource Policy (CORP) bypass. (5046d3c)
  • Validation for main thread event listener IPCs in EventRouter was strengthened to prevent a compromised renderer from spoofing extension events. (6271562)
  • Support for ML-DSA signatures in TLS has been added behind a feature flag. (a2638b6)

Web Platform & Blink

  • The CSSGapDecoration feature for styling gaps in flexbox and grid layouts has been marked as stable and is now enabled by default. (4934ed0)
  • The window.fence.notifyEvent API and related Fenced Frames code have been removed, as the feature is no longer launching. (3cca452)
  • A new tools permissions policy was introduced for the Web Model Context API (WebMCP), allowing cross-origin iframes to register and use tools when granted permission. (ea1d52c)
  • The chrome.histograms WebUI API has been updated to accept floating-point values, which are rounded to the nearest integer. This improves usability with APIs like performance.now(). (1622742)
  • Support for the spaces value in the ruby-overhang CSS property was added behind a feature flag. (85c5a07)

Android & WebView

  • The Web Feed feature has been removed, including its network/storage code and the kWebFeedContentOrder preference, as part of its ongoing deprecation. (c6e0135)
  • In WebView, Google Mobile Services (GMS) calls are now deferred until after Chromium startup by default to improve performance. This is controlled by the WebViewDeferStartupGmsCalls flag. (305be3b)
  • The Android contact picker will use the native system picker on Android 17+ when a feature flag is enabled, removing the need for the READ_CONTACTS permission. (30c0d18)
  • A prototype of a WebUI-based New Tab Page is being introduced, guarded by a GN argument and a runtime flag. (42937e4)
  • A crash in the Android BottomSheet was fixed, which occurred when changing device orientation while the keyboard was visible. (f2f79ca)

UI & UX

  • The voice search UI has been updated with a new "Aurora" recording animation and an explicit stop/submit button, gated by a feature flag. (efb148a) (6a4b098)
  • The Side Panel Bookmarks "edit dialog" was migrated from Polymer to Lit. (6786396)
  • A fix was implemented for the placement of window menus on Linux with X11, which were appearing in the wrong location due to a DIP vs. pixel coordinate mix-up. (3880f83)
  • Text annotations in the PDF viewer can now be deleted with the Backspace key in addition to the Delete key. (3790f18)
  • A race condition was fixed in tab dragging where detaching tabs could fail if the tab strip's selection model changed during the drag operation. (9000f56)

Extensions

  • A change that required a user gesture for the addHostAccessRequest API was reverted, restoring the ability for extensions to call it programmatically. (a76e95f)
  • The nacl_arch property in the runtime.getPlatformInfo() API has been deprecated, as Native Client (NaCl) was removed in M139. (6f14e2a)

Code Health & Refactoring

  • The browser_navigator files have been moved from chrome/browser/ui/ to a new chrome/browser/ui/navigator/ subdirectory, requiring updates to include paths. (815c5cf)
  • Code related to glic and lens has been modularized out of the main //chrome/browser target into their own source sets to improve dependency management. (4646bd8) (bc61a54)
  • A large number of tests in ash/ were migrated away from using the generic RunUntilIdle() to more targeted and reliable synchronization primitives, improving test stability. (78dd3d4) (60e3a08) (53b6e88)
  • A new SQL-based shared HTTP cache backend was implemented to map NetworkIsolationKey to a database ID, part of ongoing networking stack improvements. (7a02630)

2026-04-30

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-30

Overview

This summary covers 745 commits on the main branch, with 556 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from April 29, 2026 ((084453c)) to April 30, 2026 ((5186844)). The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes across the stack, impacting public C++, Mojo, and component APIs.

Major Breaking Changes & API Removals

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The WebUI Tab Strip feature has been completely removed. Builds enabling this feature via the enable_webui_tab_strip GN argument will fail. All associated files, including tab_strip.mojom and tab_strip_api.mojom, have been deleted. (17de746)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public AIProofreader mojom API has been changed to support batching. GetCorrectionType was removed and replaced by GetCorrectionsTypes, which has a different signature. (b8be3ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core webapps::ManifestId type was refactored from a GURL alias to a webapps::ValidManifestId wrapper class. This breaks all call sites that assumed it was a GURL, requiring code changes to handle the new type and its validation logic. (73319ea)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public, exported function base::DateIntervalFormat was removed from the base library. Any code relying on this function will fail to compile. (1c49b4d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The webstorePrivate extension API permission has been removed. This is a public API removal that could break external extensions that were on the allowlist. (de56b30)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The non-WebRTC transport protocol support in Remoting has been removed. The public SessionConfig and CandidateSessionConfig classes and associated methods have been deleted from remoting protocol interfaces, breaking custom transport integrations. (32d22ac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public utility function startup::ValidateUrl has been renamed to startup::ValidateLaunchUrl. Callers must be updated to use the new name. (133f8ae)

Blink, Rendering & GPU

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public C++ API for VulkanImage::Create and VulkanImage::CreateWithExternalMemory has been changed. The extra_image_create_info and extra_memory_allocation_info parameters were removed. (27868bf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public API of SharedImageStub has been reduced. The method GetSharedImageDestructionCallback and type alias SharedImageDestructionCallback were removed, and CreateSharedImage and UpdateSharedImage are now private. (e7206ff)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: An overload of the public, exported function NextPositionOf has been removed from Blink's core editing header visible_units.h as part of a larger refactoring. (57595fe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The response callback has been removed from the public Mojo method FrameWidgetInputHandler::PasteFromImageBytes, making image insertion a silent-fail operation. (6f76ae0)
  • The HarfBuzz text shaping library was rolled forward, incorporating a new hb-gpu module for GPU glyph encoding. (1ceca47)
  • The focusgroup feature was updated to exclude top-layer elements like popovers from ancestor navigation, and a bug was fixed for tabbing out of elements with tabindex=-1. (df80c56) (135c5a3)
  • For privacy, visited link colors are now ignored for elements within a canvas subtree. (8d8719a)
  • The Vertical Tabs feature is now enabled by default on Windows, Mac, and Linux. (20e810a)

Services & Components

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method send_tab_to_self::SendTabToSelfModel::AddEntry was renamed to SendEntry. Embedders using this component must update their code. (2576012)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The pure virtual method GetPlusAddressHatsData was removed from the public plus_addresses::PlusAddressService interface as part of a feature removal. Implementations of this service will need to be updated. (2465284)
  • BREAKING: The signature of the public observer method PageContentExtractionService::Observer::OnPageContentExtracted has changed. Existing implementations will break and must be updated to handle the new PageContent variant. (2f7a652)
  • Several components, including the GPU ProgramCache and Omnibox OnDeviceTailModelService, were migrated from the deprecated base::MemoryPressureListener to the new base::MemoryConsumer API. (1c39e4f) (6c95781)
  • The core browser navigator logic was extracted into a dedicated //chrome/browser/ui/navigator build target to improve modularity. (a7ea861)

Networking & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A new required method, OnPlatformLocalNetworkPermissionRequired, has been added to the public URLLoaderNetworkServiceObserver Mojo interface. This will cause compilation failures for existing implementers, who must now handle Local Network Access (LNA) permission requests. (3218389)
  • A significant security fix addresses a CSP bypass in Manifest V2 extensions where directives like script-src-elem were not properly sanitized, potentially allowing remote code execution. (99e2591)
  • To prevent information leaks, URL fragments, usernames, and passwords are now stripped from Cross-Origin Embedder Policy (COEP) and Document Isolation Policy (DIP) violation reports. (59bf0e2)
  • A data leak bypass was fixed for file selections from guest VMs (Crostini) by correctly setting the dialog caller context, ensuring Data Loss Prevention (DLP) checks are applied. (e52de61)
  • A Web Share API vulnerability was fixed, preventing a page from sharing file: URLs by manipulating the <base> tag. (65fe959)

Extensions & Media

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Support for CDM Host versions older than 10 has been removed, simplifying the Clear Key CDM implementation. This may break integrations relying on older CDM host versions. (dc13d18)
  • The MIME handler infrastructure for extensions was refactored to route generic handlers through the OOPIF (Out-of-Process iframe) path, improving security and isolation. (d680baa) (e8aa93f)
  • A bug was fixed where a renderer process could remove a webRequest listener belonging to another renderer; removals are now correctly scoped to the originating process. (be668fb)
  • The Widevine CDM for Linux arm64 is now bundled with the browser. (e94ddd2)

Platform-Specific

  • Android: The AutocompleteController API was updated to accept a WebContents parameter, enabling more contextual suggestions. (2b80341) (c96df14) (4924e16)
  • Android: Navigation logic was changed to prevent opening new tabs in non-normal windows (like PWAs or popups), falling back to a tabbed browser window instead. (d03f492)
  • Android: A memory leak in ActivityLifecycleDispatcherImpl was fixed by ensuring all ObserverList fields are cleared during dispatchOnDestroy. (f944c9f)
  • iOS: The Composebox (AIM) feature received numerous updates, including model/tool selection UI, state integration, and bug fixes. (fe189dc) (6eb4b1c)
  • iOS: The Tab Switcher Overflow Menu is now enabled by default. (0113879) (d4b0cb7)
  • ChromeOS: For enterprise users, the Local Password and PIN (LPP) feature flags were enabled. (8741038)

2026-04-29

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-29

Overview

A busy day with 699 total commits, of which 518 were considered relevant after filtering. The day was marked by a very high number of breaking changes to core APIs in areas like the compositor, UI, base library, and networking. Major refactors also continued in the layout engine and browser-finding logic. The first commit was (9bc9e6b) and the last was (7eab0f4).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING In Blink's layout engine, several public virtual methods on LayoutBoxModelObject (e.g., BorderTop(), PaddingRight()) have been removed. Code must be updated to use the consolidated BorderOutsets() and PaddingOutsets() methods. (b2246d7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Key public virtual methods on the compositor's LayerTreeHostImpl (e.g., BeginCommit, CreatePendingTree) have been moved to a new ClientLayerTreeHostImpl subclass. Embedders who subclass or directly call these methods must update to the new class. (144eb65)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for SharedImageFactory::CreateSharedImage has changed. It now accepts a SharedImageInfo struct instead of a long list of parameters. (08ee0a7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public methods related to fullscreen state (e.g., ShouldHideUIForFullscreen()) have been removed from the BrowserWindow and Browser interfaces. This functionality now resides in the new BrowserWindowFullscreenController. (d1db08e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used function chrome::FindBrowserWithTab is being removed. Embedders must migrate to new APIs like GlobalBrowserCollection to find a Browser* from a WebContents*. (48bd889)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::RandInt function has been removed from base/rand_util.h. Callers must migrate to base::RandIntInclusive. (5f591f6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The password_manager::PasswordStoreConsumer interface was changed, replacing the virtual method OnGetPasswordStoreResults with OnGetPasswordStoreResultsOrErrorFrom. (18f3b6c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method writable_hdr_metadata() has been removed from the media::VideoDecoderConfig class. (5794b9e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The MADV_FREE-based discardable memory feature, including the public function base::GetDiscardableMemoryBacking(), has been removed from base. (73c9221)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Legacy non-WebRTC remoting components have been removed, including public headers and the CreateChannelAuthenticator() pure virtual method from remoting::protocol::Authenticator. (e548872)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public RTCLoggingDispatcher Mojo interface was modified. FinishDiagnosticLogging now requires a metadata parameter, and the Mojo file's directory was renamed, breaking include paths. (8e0ea31)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the SideUiObserver interface method onPreSideUiSpecsChange now returns an android.transition.Transition instead of an android.animation.Animator. (3f4d377)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public AndroidAutofillClient class now inherits from content::WebContentsObserver, altering its class hierarchy and requiring subclasses to handle new lifecycle methods. (7ae919c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The behavior of RTCPeerConnection has changed with the enforcement of the Connection-Allowlist header. Construction can now be blocked by this header, which may require server-side configuration changes. (ec55185)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The behavior of ProtocolHandlerRegistry for Off-The-Record (OTR) profiles has changed. The registry is no longer shared with the original profile, isolating handlers. (7495f64)

Security

  • The RejectWeakCiphertext feature, which blocks weak symmetric ciphers in TLS, is now enabled by default. (5b951ab)
  • A logic flaw in DevTools that could leak raw header access and HttpOnly cookies after a session was detached has been fixed. (b1cb06e)
  • The Omnibox SearchSuggestionParser now validates that navigation suggestions use only HTTP or HTTPS schemes, preventing execution of malicious javascript: URLs. (f1feef3)
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in AXTreeSourceAndroid was fixed by ensuring a raw pointer is not retained across tree mutations that could invalidate it. (55bfe49)
  • Input validation was added to the VAAPI video decoders and encoders for AV1, H264, and VP8 to reject malformed data and prevent potential crashes or vulnerabilities. (e616e27) (1647d70) (390e0ed)

Blink & Web Platform

  • Support was added for calc() expressions that mix <angle> and <percentage> types in conic-gradient() color stops, aligning with the CSS Images 4 specification. (2f0622a)
  • The CSS line-clamp feature had its continue and block-ellipsis keywords renamed to align with new specification resolutions. (10dbb90)
  • A new kWebNNModelCompilation sandbox type was added for the WebNN API. This will be used for an upcoming compiler utility process to isolate ONNX Runtime graph compilation. (1adb86c)
  • The streaming sanitizer was updated to better handle text node normalization, aligning its behavior more closely with the HTML parser. (5aa3eb2)

UI & UX

  • The API to play specific segments of a Lottie animation (playSegments) has been made public, allowing for more granular control over animations. (bc01b08)
  • A tab's memory usage is now displayed on the tab hover card UI. (605b071)
  • A new feature flag, kWebUIOmniboxFullPopupV2, was introduced to allow parallel development on a next-generation WebUI omnibox popup. (099d60d)
  • The TranslateLanguageListModel interface was updated to expose language codes via GetTargetLanguageCodeAt, preparing for a new searchable language selection UI. (210dc00)

Extensions

  • Support for adding custom context menu items to the browser's tab context menu has been implemented. Extensions with the contextMenus permission can specify "tab" as a context, enabled by the kExtensionTabContextMenu feature flag. (ad49992)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Method signatures in the public extensions::ProcessMap API were changed to use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of int. (2686e85)
  • A use-after-free vulnerability in UserScriptSet::UpdateUserScripts was fixed by ensuring memory is handled correctly during script updates. (fe455e2)

Mobile (Android & iOS)

  • On Android, the UnthrottleAsyncTouchMoves feature is now enabled by default (excluding WebView) to improve gesture responsiveness. (d40e41a)
  • On Android, the location bar was refactored, centralizing phone-specific button logic into the LocationBarCoordinator. (2d80323) (cf69f5d) (829b178)
  • On iOS, support for native map previews via universal links has been added. (0440587)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On iOS, the public virtual method BuildExternalPrivacyContext was removed from the SystemIdentityManager class. (6246ffa)

Code Removals & Infrastructure

  • Support for the pre-mmap Safe Browsing file format has been removed. Instead of migrating, Chromium will now return an error, prompting a full database update. (158b83a)
  • The Single Web Feed feature, previously used for the Creator Page in the Feed, has been removed along with its associated code and metrics. (7532c85)
  • The build system's proto_library template was updated to publicly propagate dependencies, fixing build issues where transitive headers could not be found. (7107857)
  • The unused translation_unit tool has been removed from the codebase. (0f53c60)
Page 1 of 2
